qa_pairs.question: What is a storage allocation?
qa_pairs.answer: Storage allocations are the most straightforward to understand: research groups will get a maximum amount of storage that they can use exclusively throughout the allocation period.

qa_pairs.question: How are core year and GPU year allocations described?
qa_pairs.answer: Core year and GPU year allocations are more difficult to understand because these allocations are meant to capture average use throughout the allocation period – typically meant to be a year – and this use will occur across a set of resources shared with other research groups.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more information about compute allocations?
qa_pairs.answer: To learn more about compute allocations, read the [[Allocations and compute scheduling]] documentation.

qa_pairs.question: When are successful allocations activated?
qa_pairs.answer: Generally, all successful allocations are activated in April. The exact starting date will be confirmed in your notification letter.
